items.title: Vets turn away wild birds as avian influenza detected in SA

children.content: Wildlife rescuers say they need help to prepare for a potential bird flu outbreak as vets issue statements saying they can no longer take in sick or injured wild birds.

articles.title: Sea bird sick with H5 avian flu landed by bodyboarders, too weak to fly to shore

children.content: A surfer who spotted the first bird in South Australia to test positive for H5N1 bird flu says the giant petrel looked fatigued and distressed before it was retrieved by wildlife rescuers.

image.altText: A man with white hair and a black hoodie smiles at the camera

articles.title: Can vaccines stop deadly bird flu from spreading?

children.content: With bird flu arriving on our shores, governments, wildlife groups and agricultural organisations are springing into action. But can vaccines limit the economic and environmental damage of the deadly influenza?

image.altText: A veterinarian injects a vaccine into a chick in an industry chicken farm

items.title: Antic's 'dangerous' comments on vaccinations condemned

children.content: South Australian Liberal senator Alex Antic has been roundly condemned over comments he made about vaccinations after avian influenza was found on Australia's southern coast.

image.altText: A close up of Alex Antic speaking in the Senate chamber, in a suit with a red tie

items.title: NBL fines Bogut over alleged body-shaming comments to 36ers owner

children.content: The NBL hands Andrew Bogut a fine of $9,500 over comments he allegedly made towards Adelaide 36ers owner Grant Kelley.

image.altText: A man looks a little bit annoyed while watching a basketball game from the sidelines.

items.title: SA nurses and midwives offered 16 per cent pay rise under new deal

children.content: Nurses and midwives in South Australia would receive a 16 per cent pay rise by mid-2028 under a new deal endorsed by their union and the state government.

image.altText: A woman wearing a blue blazer looking at the camera

items.title: Triple fatality prompts medical certificate call for learner drivers

children.content: South Australia's deputy coroner recommends learner drivers obtain a medical certificate affirming their ability to drive before they get a licence.

image.altText: A woman leans against an older man in a hat and striped shirt and an older woman wearing glasses, all smiling.

items.title: Understanding of harmful algal blooms still murky, disaster survey finds

children.content: Most Australians remain uncertain about the cause of harmful algal blooms and are more worried about disasters such as bushfires and flooding.

gallery.altText: Piles of dead fish on a beach at sunset

items.title: SA council elections delayed amid review into March poll

children.content: The state government has passed legislation to delay South Australia's council elections until April 2027, after the Electoral Commission raised "very serious concerns" about its ability to administer the poll in November.

image.altText: A metal sign that says 'Salisbury Community Hub' with a tv screen reading 'City of Salisbury' with blue logo.

items.title: Animal cruelty charges laid a year on from piggery footage

children.content: A year after footage from the site was widely circulated the RSPCA has pressed animal cruelty charges against people connected to Andgar Piggery.

items.title: Woman fights to have ex-policeman husband kept behind bars

children.content: A domestic violence survivor speaks out about her fight to keep her former detective ex-husband behind bars as he attempts to be released on parole.

image.altText: A woman with black haair close-up looking sad

items.title: Kangaroo Island declared feral pig-free after more than two centuries

children.content: Devastating bushfires provide Kangaroo Island with a unique silver lining as it becomes the largest island in the world to eradicate feral pigs.

items.title: 'Significant errors' in 'preventable' death of baby in murder-suicide

children.content: A coroner finds the "upsetting" death of a nine-month-old baby in the Barossa Valley in a public murder-suicide by her "manipulative, self-absorbed and dangerous" father could have been prevented.

articles.title: A failed forest utopia will watch nervously as the Socceroos play Paraguay

items.title: Mixed reaction to beach town at forefront of over-50s lifestyle resort boom

items.title: Abalone divers say 41 per cent catch reduction 'a kick in the face'

items.title: Ratepayers fork out $1.2m to help solve WA's three-bin problem

children.content: Organic waste from WA's second-largest city will be trucked to Perth at the cost to ratepayers of $1.2 million a year, following the collapse of local processing.
